biodynamic.name: Niepoort
biodynamic.address: Quinta de Napoles, Tabuaco, 5120 Cima Corgo, Portugal
biodynamic.description: Niepoort has farmed organically since 1999 and applies biodynamic practices across its Douro estates, though not all parcels are formally certified.
biodynamic.tip: Niepoort applies biodynamic methods without promoting full Demeter certification; ask at the cellar which parcels are farmed biodynamically.

organic.name: Quinta do Orgal (Quinta do Vallado)
organic.address: Castelo Melhor, 5150 Vila Nova de Foz Coa, Portugal
organic.description: Vallado's Douro Superior property at Castelo Melhor has 35-plus hectares of organic vineyards, certified organic in February 2015 with effect from 2012.
organic.tip: The Quinta do Orgal Organic Vineyards bottling is the estate's certified-organic Douro Superior red; look for it specifically.

organic.name: Quinta do Tedo
organic.description: The Bouchard family's 18th-century estate at the meeting of the Tedo and Douro rivers grows organic grapes and runs hands-on harvest foot-treading experiences.
organic.tip: Combine an organic-estate visit with a September lagar foot-treading session; the family welcomes guests into the harvest.

organic.name: Niepoort organic vineyards
organic.address: Quinta de Napoles, Tabuaco, 5120 Cima Corgo, Portugal
organic.description: Niepoort owns 60-plus hectares in the Douro farmed organically since 1999, many parcels certified organic, with low-intervention winemaking on old-vine field blends.
organic.tip: Many but not all Niepoort parcels are certified; the Bioma and Redoma bottlings come from the organic core of the estate.

natural.name: Folias de Baco (Uivo)
natural.description: Tiago Sampaio makes the Uivo wines from high-altitude vines near Alijo with no added yeast, no fining and no filtration, a benchmark for natural wine in the Douro.
natural.tip: The Uivo range, from skin-contact whites to the Renegado field blend, is the place to start with natural Douro wine.

vegan_winemaking.name: Quinta do Crasto
vegan_winemaking.neighborhood: Cima Corgo, between Regua and Pinhao
vegan_winemaking.description: Several Quinta do Crasto wines, including the Crasto red, the Superior Branco and the old-vine reservas, are made without animal-derived fining and are certified vegan.
vegan_winemaking.tip: Crasto does not hold organic certification, but its core reds and whites are vegan-friendly; check the back label for the vegan mark.

vegan_winemaking.name: Niepoort Redoma
vegan_winemaking.address: Quinta de Napoles, Tabuaco, 5120 Cima Corgo, Portugal
vegan_winemaking.description: Niepoort's Redoma Tinto is an organic, unfined and unfiltered Douro red made without animal-derived fining and marked vegan.
vegan_winemaking.tip: Redoma comes in red, white and rose; the unfined, unfiltered reds are the clearest expression of Niepoort's vegan, low-intervention approach.

lowsulfite.name: Folias de Baco low-sulfite wines
lowsulfite.description: Tiago Sampaio bottles the Uivo wines with very low sulfites, around 20 ppm total for the whites, with no fining and no filtration, among the lowest in the Douro.
lowsulfite.tip: Because sulfites are so low, store and serve these wines cool; they reward fresh, prompt drinking.

lowsulfite.name: Niepoort Redoma low-sulfite reds
lowsulfite.address: Quinta de Napoles, Tabuaco, 5120 Cima Corgo, Portugal
lowsulfite.description: Niepoort's low-intervention Redoma reds are made organically with minimal added sulfur, unfined and unfiltered, low for a serious age-worthy Douro red.
lowsulfite.tip: Redoma shows that low-sulfite handling and cellar-worthy structure are not mutually exclusive; these reds age well.
